task_completion.description: Identify the process holding the volume open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'lsof /home/ga/MountPoints/secure_logs' or 'fuser -m /home/ga/MountPoints/secure_logs' being executed, with output revealing the PID and the command name 'tail'.

task_completion.description: Create the incident report file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal command (like echo, cat, or nano) creating or opening '/home/ga/Documents/lock_incident.txt'.

task_completion.description: The incident report contains the identified PID and Command Name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of '/home/ga/Documents/lock_incident.txt' are visible (e.g., in a text editor or via 'cat') and explicitly include the PID found in the earlier step and the command name 'tail'.

task_completion.description: Terminate the locking process or prepare a force dismount.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a 'kill <PID>' or 'killall tail' command targeting the identified process, or a dismount command using a force flag (e.g., 'umount -f' or 'veracrypt -d -f').

Terminal showing a successful dismount command (e.g., 'veracrypt -d /home/ga/Volumes/log_storage.hc' or 'umount /home/ga/MountPoints/secure_logs') executing without 'target is busy' errors, or the VeraCrypt GUI showing the volume is no longer mounted.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely identified the PID rather than hallucinating it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PID written in the report must match the PID shown in the output of the diagnostic command (e.g., lsof or fuser) executed by the agent.

integrity.description: The incident report was actually saved to the disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the file is saved (no unsaved indicator), or a terminal command like 'cat' or 'ls' confirms the file exists at '/home/ga/Documents/lock_incident.txt' with the correct contents.

integrity.description: The dismount action was actually executed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dismount command was submitted in the terminal (showing a new prompt line after execution) or the GUI dismount button was clicked and processed, not just typed out without execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The volume is located at '/home/ga/Volumes/log_storage.hc' and mounted at '/home/ga/MountPoints/secure_logs'. The locking process is spawned using 'tail' (specifically 'tail -f' on a file in the mount point). The incident report must be located at '/home/ga/Documents/lock_incident.txt' and must contain the correct PID (which the agent must discover dynamically) and the command name 'tail'.
